introduction: Compound Interest (CI) is interest calculated on the principal plus accumulated interest from earlier periods. Each period the base grows, so interest increases over time unlike simple interest. Exams test annual compounding, half-yearly compounding, finding rate or time, and comparing CI with SI.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

For annual compounding, Amount A = P(1 + R/100)^T and Compound Interest CI = A − P.

When compounding is half-yearly, the rate per period becomes R/2 and the number of periods becomes 2T: A = P(1 + R/200)^(2T).

For quarterly compounding, use R/4 and 4T similarly.

Useful result: difference between CI and SI on the same sum for 2 years at R% annually is P(R/100)^2.

For 3 years, CI − SI = P(R/100)^2 × (3 + R/100) when compounded annually.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Bank savings accounts, fixed deposits with compounding, loan EMI structures, and investment growth projections commonly use compound interest.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Forgetting to double periods when compounding half-yearly.
2) Using R instead of R/2 in the half-yearly formula.
3) Subtracting principal incorrectly when only CI is required.
4) Applying simple interest for multi-year compounding problems.
5) Rounding intermediate values too early in multi-step calculations.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• CI > SI for the same P, R, T when T > 1 year (annual compounding).

• For 2 years, CI − SI = P(R/100)^2 exactly.

• Half-yearly: n = 2T periods, rate per period = R/2%.

• If amount doubles, (1 + R/100)^T = 2; solve for unknown R or T.

• Effective annual rate exceeds nominal rate when compounding is more frequent than yearly.

formulas.title: CI minus SI (2 years)
formulas.content: CI − SI = P(R/100)^2 for annual compounding

formulas.title: Two-year amount shortcut
formulas.content: A = P[1 + 2R/100 + (R/100)^2] when T = 2 years compounded annually

shortcutTips.title: Half-yearly rule
shortcutTips.tip: Split rate by 2 and multiply time periods by 2 for half-yearly compounding.

shortcutTips.title: Two-year CI−SI
shortcutTips.tip: For 2 years at R%, CI exceeds SI by P(R/100)^2 without full expansion.

shortcutTips.title: Successive multipliers
shortcutTips.tip: Multiply by (1 + R/100) once per compounding period instead of recomputing from scratch.

shortcutTips.title: CI from amount
shortcutTips.tip: Always subtract principal after calculating amount to get compound interest.

shortcutTips.title: Compare growth
shortcutTips.tip: For T = 1 year, CI equals SI under annual compounding.

shortcutTips.title: Square small rates
shortcutTips.tip: When R = 10%, use (1.1)^T; for T = 2, multiplier is 1.21 directly.

solvedExamples.problem: Find the compound interest on Rs 10,000 at 10% per annum for 2 years compounded annually.
Find the amount on Rs 8,000 at 5% per annum for 2 years compounded half-yearly.
Difference between CI and SI on Rs 5,000 at 10% for 2 years is:
At what rate percent will Rs 16,000 amount to Rs 18,816 in 2 years compounded annually?
